claude-opus-4-6.name: Claude Opus 4.6
promptGuidelines: Use XML tags to structure prompts: <task>, <context>, <constraints>, <examples>
Leverage adaptive thinking mode — the model adjusts reasoning depth automatically
Use 'consider' or 'evaluate' instead of 'think' when extended thinking is disabled
Provide positive AND negative examples to guide behavior precisely
For complex tasks, nest XML tags: <identity> for role, <task> for instructions, <rules> for constraints
Specify effort level expectations: high for complex reasoning, medium for balanced speed/quality
Supports 1M context (beta) — excellent for very large codebases and documents
Be explicit about tool use triggers — avoid 'MUST use' or 'NEVER use', prefer conditional guidance

claude-sonnet-4-5.name: Claude Sonnet 4.5
promptGuidelines: Use XML tags for structure: <task>, <context>, <output_format>
System prompts are highly effective — define tone, behavior, and defaults
Supports extended thinking mode for multi-step reasoning
Best balance of speed and intelligence for routine coding tasks
Provide examples to indicate desired format and style
Keep prompts focused — Sonnet excels when instructions are clear and direct

claude-haiku-4-5.name: Claude Haiku 4.5
promptGuidelines: Keep prompts SHORT and CONCISE — Haiku is optimized for speed
Use for classification, extraction, and quick Q&A tasks
Supports extended thinking (first Haiku with this capability)
Ideal for multi-agent pipelines where latency matters
Avoid overly complex nested structures — simple XML tags work best
Fast enough for real-time interactive applications

gemini-3-pro.name: Gemini 3 Pro
promptGuidelines: Gemini 3 is LESS VERBOSE by default — explicitly request detailed responses if needed
Use XML tags (<role>, <instructions>, <constraints>) or Markdown headings for structure
Place all context FIRST, then instructions at the end with 'Based on the above...'
Use split-step verification: first verify info exists, then generate the answer
For complex tasks, chain prompts — output of one becomes input of next
Define verbosity level explicitly: Low/Medium/High in constraints
System instructions should combine: role definition + step-by-step plan + constraints
Prioritize critical instructions at the very beginning of the prompt

gemini-2-5-flash.name: Gemini 2.5 Flash
promptGuidelines: Fast response with massive 1M token context window
Use thinking mode for reasoning tasks — set level to LOW for speed
Structure prompts with clear sections using XML or Markdown
Good balance of speed and capability for high-throughput applications
Provide few-shot examples for specific formatting requirements
Use 'think silently' in system instructions for lower latency

gemini-2-5-pro.name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
promptGuidelines: Enhanced thinking capabilities — great for complex analysis and code generation
Structure prompts with XML tags for best results
Supply context first, then ask questions at the end
Use few-shot examples to indicate desired output format (JSON, code, etc.)
Large 1M context allows processing entire codebases
Be direct and precise — avoid unnecessary or persuasive language

codex-5-3.name: Codex 5.3
promptGuidelines: Give HIGH-LEVEL intent sketches — Codex is tuned for imprecise developer prompts
Designed for long-running autonomous tasks — describe the goal, not each step
Steer during execution without losing context — works like a colleague
Excellent at multi-step debugging, code review, and large codebase navigation
Specify scope explicitly: which files, which functions, what constraints
Excels at agentic workflows — delegate subtasks and let it plan
Top performance on vulnerability discovery and exploitation tasks

codex-5-3-spark.name: Codex 5.3 Spark
promptGuidelines: Use SHORT, TARGETED prompts — Spark defaults to minimal changes
Designed for real-time interactive coding: edits, logic tweaks, interface refinement
Ultra-fast: 1000+ tokens/second — ideal for rapid iteration loops
Explicitly request tests if needed — Spark won't auto-generate them
Best for: targeted function edits, quick logic adjustments, interface refinement
Not suited for long-horizon autonomous tasks — use Codex 5.3 for those
Interrupt and redirect mid-output for tight feedback loops

plan.name: Plan
plan.systemPromptAddition: Focus on high-level architecture, system design, and planning. Break down complex problems into manageable components. Consider trade-offs and provide rationale for decisions.
guidelines: Include diagrams or pseudocode when helpful

edit.name: Edit
edit.systemPromptAddition: Focus on precise code modifications. Provide complete, working code that follows best practices. Include necessary imports and handle edge cases.

review.name: Review
review.systemPromptAddition: Analyze code for bugs, security issues, performance problems, and style violations. Provide specific, actionable feedback with line references.

ask.name: Ask
ask.systemPromptAddition: Provide clear, educational explanations. Use examples to illustrate concepts. Adjust complexity based on the apparent skill level of the question.
